observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Undertake the procedures indicated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account to enable the LGU to come up with reliable PPE account. Considering the bulk of total PPE, the agency may consider requesting one-time cleansing per account. Indicate in the request for the one-time cleansing the ₱58.314 million unaccounted forwarded cost; 
Re-evaluate the assignment of personnel especially of job order workers for possible availability of personnel who can assist in backtracking of records; and
Regularly conduct the physical count of property and equipment and immediately prepare and submit the required report.
observations.reason: The management has not yet established an inventory committee and no physical count of PPE conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management immediately investigate the noted discrepancies starting from the latest financial statements and prepare necessary adjusting entries to immediately correct the errors incurred in recording. 
We also recommend to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the forwarded balance which does not have any records available on file to inform the users of the financial records of such matter and to properly guide them in their decision making.
observations.reason: Not imp | Non-reconciliation of GPPA monitoring between Accounting and Engineering prior to submission to COA

observations.managementAction:
A memorandum has been issued by MACCO, indicating that the Municipal Accounting Office will no longer allow the deduction of loans to the salaries of employees if their monthly net take home pay will fall below P5,000.00. 

In addition, the Management sent out letters addressed to private banks regarding their intention to terminate the Memorandum of Agreement to prevent the employees from availing further loans.
observations.reason: Some employees continue to have a net take home pay below the prescribed amount due to their outstanding loans, which will remain in effect until the end of their loan term.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately investigate the noted discrepancies starting from the latest financial statements and prepare necessary adjusting entries to immediately correct the errors incurred in recording. Also, for the succeeding periods, conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and immediately investigate and correct noted deficiencies to avoid accumulation of unaccounted amounts.

We recommended that Management prohibit delayed cash advance settlements such as payment on staggard basis or balances deducted to forthcoming advances; immediate liquidation shall be demanded. 

We also recommend that Management assign personnel who will assist Accounting Office in monitoring cash advance and exhaustion of possible means to collect outstanding cash advances from personnel no longer connected to the LGU. Immediate settlement of the above-mentioned cash advances totaling ₱₱355,510.31 shall be made within fifteen calendar days from receipt of this audit memorandum. Non-settlement by respective accountable officers will constitute application of Item 9.0 of COA Circular 2012-004.

We recommended that Management:

Conduct an inventory of drugs and medicines and recognize these assets in the books of the LGU. For donated drugs and medicines as well as unrecorded medical supplies, refer to Property Transfer Report and other equivalent documents in recognizing the cost of these assets. In the absence of indicated cost in the said reports, coordinate with the source agencies. 
Use the asset method in recording purchased medicines. To recognize distributed drugs and medicines, the Rural Health Unit should submit a month-end report indicating the quantity and cost of distributed medicines. 
Direct the Rural Health Unit to assign a personnel in-charge of inventory management including but not limited to maintaining an active records of drugs and medicines received, distributed and balance on-hand including the expiration dates of such, conduct random inventory counting of drugs and medicines and reconcile with records. Immediately distribute medicines that are nearly expiring and immediately dispose expired medicines; and 
To centralize distribution of medicines only through the Pharmacy, if found impracticable, assigned personnel has to maintain similar records prescribed by the above DOH manual and alongside submit to the assigned pharmacy in charge for the consolidation of medicine and supplies inventory report.

observations.managementAction: No separate Financial Statements prepared and submitted.
observations.reason: No separate Financial Statements prepared and submitted.
ot implem

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
1.10.1 Create an inventory committee and undertake the procedures indicated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account to enable the LGU to come up with reliable PPE account. Considering the bulk of its PPE, the agency may consider requesting one-time cleansing per account. Indicate in the request for the one-time cleansing the amount of ₱59.956 million which are unaccounted forwarded cost.
Re-evaluate the assignment of personnel especially of job order workers for possible availability of personnel who can assist in backtracking of records.
Regularly conduct the physical count of property and equipment and immediately prepare and submit the required report.

We recommended and the Management agreed to immediately investigate the noted discrepancies starting from the latest financial statements and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors incurred in recording. Also, for the succeeding periods, conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and immediately investigate and correct noted deficiencies to avoid accumulation of unaccounted amounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the LDRRMO to prepare the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose the LDRRM Funds in the Notes to Financial Statement in conformity with the prescribed format in Annexes A and D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;
Require the LDRRMO to furnish the OCD and DILG-LGOO as well as COA a copy each of the annual final LDRRMFIP and the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified by the Municipal Accountant as prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that transfers of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the Trust Fund are recorded correctly in the books of accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management accepted that the Management prioritize the reconciliation of the remittance files with the billing files to address the outstanding discrepancies. Re-evaluate the tasks of LGU personnel particularly the job order workers and assign personnel who will concentrate on document retrieval and updating of service records. Closely coordinate with the GSIS and submit the necessary service records and remittance files to substantiate the remittances made.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, to re-evaluate their Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan to include projects and activities that would address the needs of the locality and regularly monitor the status of program implementation to timely identify areas that needs improvement;
Consider succession planning in the manning of personnel particularly in the LDRRM Office to avoid impairing the ability of office to deliver services in case of personnel turn-over; and
For the LDRRMC to conduct regular evaluation of the status of implementation of the planned programs and activities to timely address any issues that could affect its implementation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to, through the Accounting Office, evaluate their current practice and identify areas which can be improved to ensure that financial and transaction reports will be timely submitted for audit, which may include provision of additional manpower to the accounting office.

observations.managementAction:
Certification from Land Bank of the Philippines was secured indicating that LGU Lagonglong has no existing loans payable under the MDFO account. 

Previously MDFO funded loans were assigned to Land Bank of the Philippines per Section 5.3 of DOF Department Circular No. 006.2020

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management regularly prepare individual contract of service for each job order worker specifying the individual rate, the contract period, the individual duties and responsibilities, and any other stipulations found necessary to be indicated in the contract.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented quarterly renewal of Job Order contract, and individual contracts were prepared and given to the Job Order workers, indicating the necessary stipulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Ensure compliance and adherence with R.A. No. 6713 and Section 89 of R.A. No. 7160 to prevent transactions prohibited by conflicts of interest; and
Refrain from making purchases through EGYY Digital Solutions; and ensure that all necessary details, such as the date received and the period of delivery, are fully completed for Contracts, Purchase Orders, Inspection and Acceptance Reports, Notice to Proceed, and Notice of Award.
observations.managementAction: No further purchases were made by the Municipality to EGYY Digital Solutions in 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the concerned officials:

To see to it that the excessive cost/negative variance will be deducted from the succeeding claims or any amount due to contractors and/or deduct it from the retention money or other securities posted by contractors, whichever is applicable and submit proof of deductions in compliance with the Technical results;
Submit the required documents and/or explanation/justifications, if any, to facilitate re-evaluation of the technical aspects of the contracts; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DPWH Department Orders in the preparation of the ABC, POW, Plans and Drawings to avoid errors in the quantities, measurements and descriptions. Otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management fully accomplish the purchase orders; indicate when the purchase order was made, the mode of procurement used, and more particularly the date of delivery. 

We further recommended to consistently ensure that all purchase orders were duly received by the supplier and the date of receipt clearly indicated in the face of the purchase order.
observations.managementAction:
The management issued a memorandum to emphasize the importance of thorough and accurate completion of purchase requests and purchase orders. 
While subsequent purchase orders are properly accomplished, with all the necessary details, they have not been submitted to COA within the five-day period following the perfection of the PO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management through the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:

Discontinue the practice of keeping the Barangay fund in trust for the procurement of accountable forms. Barangays shall directly procure accountable forms from the Municipality
Simplify the recording process by maintaining a single record per accountable forms. Procurement of the Barangays shall be taken out from this list and details of the requisitioner including the name of the Barangay date of release shall be properly written in the logbook for proper identification
observations.managementAction: Old accountable forms of barangays awaiting to be fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended to discontinue the practice of reimbursement of fuel expenses except for those inevitable, emergency, and immaterial transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to provide a legal basis for the disbursements totaling ₱161,000.00 and to discontinue payments of similar nature until sufficient legal basis is provided to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Through the accounting office, to appropriately record the Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits of the Agency;
b. Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations for the agency to have sufficient cash funding for the leave benefits of its employees. Should the management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payable, it is recommended to prioritize the current year amounts; and
c. Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the leave benefits payable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the immediate liquidation of transferred funds to concern government agencies. Order the return of unused monies. This could be initiated by the sending of letters to defaulting agencies requesting for submission of   necessary documents proving the utilization or non-use of transferred funds. In addition, to establish accurate amount of receivables, the accounting of said accounts shall be thoroughly checked as to validity which shall start with the investigation of abnormal balances.

As for the funds received by the Municipality from other government agencies which remained outstanding for more than 3 years, exhaustive effort should be made to locate the records and submit the needed documents to fully liquidate the money. If papers can no longer be recovered, request for write-off of accounts shall be sent to the COA Commission Proper so that adjustments in recording on the agency’s book of accounts may be effected.

We recommended the Management through the accounting office to prioritize tracing and locating relevant documents to serve as basis in accounting for the reconciling items, and prepare necessary adjustment.

We further recommended to conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the difference.

We recommended to the Management to:
Through the accounting office, to record the Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits of the Agency by preparing a journal entry including the prior period adjustment:
Terminal Leave Benefits 		   783,485.28
Prior Leave Benefits			29,916,466.85
Leave Benefits Payable			30,699,952.13

Should the Management find it hard to record the whole amount instantly, identify specific time frame when full amount of liability is expected to be fully recorded in the books of the agency (ex. 5-year installment basis) and ensure that sufficient amount is appropriated annually.

We recommended the Management to instruct drivers to provide accurate and complete data in the driver’s trip ticket particularly the vehicle plate number, name of passengers and the odometer reading upon exit from and return to the garage.
We also recommended to discontinue the practice of reimbursement of fuel expenses except for those inevitable, emergency, and immaterial transactions.

We recommended the Management to provide a legal basis for the disbursements totaling ₱161,000.00 and to discontinue payments of similar nature until sufficient legal basis is provided to the audit team.

We recommended to the Management:

a. For the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of the identified stale checks informing that their checks are already stale. For future transactions, written notices to the payees shall be done one month before checks become stale;
b. For the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for stale checks
i. For stale checks not over 2 years –

Cash in Bank – LCCA	xxx
	Liability Account		xxx

ii. For stale checks over 2 years –

Cash in Bank, LCCA		xxx
	Prior Years Adjustment*	xxx
	(*to represent surplus adjustment)

c. For the Municipal Accountant to regularly record the interest income incurred by trust fund accounts to the General Fund of the agency; 
d. For the Municipal Accountant to immediately record any book reconciling items appearing in the course of regular preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements to avoid its accumulation; and
e. For the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to investigate the long outstanding deposits in transit and notify the audit team as to the result of its investigation.

We recommended the Accounting Department to:

properly compute for the HDMF employee and employer share at a maximum of ₱100.00 considering the maximum monthly compensation amounting to ₱5,000.00 as mandated under Section 7 of RA No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 dated July 21, 2009; and
communicate with the HDMF on proper application or reversion of excess payment made by the agency.

We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, management direct officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the projects and activities to be funded.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor the following:
Employ measures and methods to ensure timely and sequential submission of disbursement vouchers from the Treasurer’s Office to Accounting Office and eventually to COA pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 to facilitate timely review by the Auditor;
Implement methods of communicating to the concerned payees or suppliers for the timely release of their respective checks.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly adhere to Section 475 of RA 7160 and Section 112 of PD 1445.

Recommendations:
We recommend that in order to reduce the delay in the release of barangay RPT Shares the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Closely monitor and expedite the preparation and processing of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and Payrolls and other documents pertaining to the release of RPT shares;
Coordinate in identifying other bottleneck in the process flow that caused the delay in the release of RPT shares and take remedial measures to mitigate, if not eliminate, the same.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of Sec. 272 of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.

We recommended to stop the practice of charging to LDRRM QRF expenses which are not supported by Local Sangguniang Resolution and declaration of state of calamity to adhere strictly the guidelines set under Section 2 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Management immediately increase the Fidelity Bond of the designated AO of the Municipality to amount corresponding to his maximum cash handled in accordance with the revised schedule of premium rates of fidelity bond as presented in Annex C issued by the Bureau of Treasury and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Bureau of Treasury Circular 02-2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the immediate liquidation of transferred funds to concern government agencies. Order the return of unused monies. This could be initiated by the sending of letters to defaulting agencies requesting for submission of   necessary documents proving the utilization or non-use of transferred funds. In addition, to establish accurate amount of receivables, the accounting of said accounts shall be thoroughly checked as to validity which shall start with the investigation of abnormal balances.

As for the funds received by the Municipality from other government agencies which remained outstanding for more than 3 years, exhaustive effort should be made to locate the records and submit the needed documents to fully liquidate the money. If papers can no longer be recovered, request for write-off of accounts shall be sent to the COA Commission Proper so that adjustments in recording on the agency’s book of accounts may be effected.
observations.reason:
The Current Municipal Accountant had already traced based on all records retrieved and made subsidiary ledgers for different projects. However, those untraceable amounts as inquired pertains to prior years. 

During the 2019 Exit Conference, the SA suggested that prior years’ AARs should be downloaded to reconcile the said amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to instruct the Disbursing Officer to reflect faithfully those transactions or payments which were granted as cash advances through her name, and effect reconciliation of items or figures with the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the required documents herein enumerated for review and to submit a written justification as to why it was not submitted within 5 working days from the execution of the contract by the agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should exert effort and install strategies to fast tract settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following remedial measures:

a)      Payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.

 b)    Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts
 In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.
We recommended that the LGU-Lagonglong Management take appropriate actions on these observations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
 a. That Management direct Ms. Laniton to report to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and function as the Assistant Municipal Treasurer as appointed; and

b.That Ms. Laniton refund the total RATA amount she received while performing the work other than as the Assistant Municipal Treasurer in the total amount of P78,400.00.
observations.reason: Ms. Laniton fully remitted the disallowance as of CY 2018.

observations.reason: Management already sent a communication letter to the supplier regarding the said observation but the former did not receive any feedback from the latter. Management, however, committed to follow up this matter. As of December 31, 2018, P1,210,187.50 was the remaining balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to instruct the Disbursing Officer to reflect faithfully those transactions or payments which were granted as cash advances through her name, and effect reconciliation of items or figures with the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the required documents herein enumerated for review and to submit a written justification as to why it was not submitted within 5 working days from the execution of the contract by the agency.
We recommend that Management should submit justification on the above-mentioned transactions for evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant not to process disbursement vouchers without the necessary supporting documents to support the claim in compliance with COA Circular No. 2005-03.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with release of purchase orders to furnish to the auditor copies of these documents.

Likewise, copies of the purchase orders irrespective of amount including the supporting documents should be submitted to the auditor within five days from the issuance thereof in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipality evaluate further the components included in its 20% annual internal revenue allotment (IRA) for development projects to avoid incurring expenditures that are not related to and/or not connected with the implementation of development projects, programs and activities of the Municipality in conformity with the Joint DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular.

We recommended therefore that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated DRRM Officer to prioritize the preparation and submission of the LDRRMFIP, and the maintenance of public awareness through bulletin board.  The submission, and compliance of, these reports has basic importance on the lives of the constituents as it promotes disaster mitigation, prevention, and preparedness. 

In addition, the Designated DRRM Officer must consider the prompt fulfillment of the activities or tasks enumerated in the LDRRMFIP as the most effective way of handling disaster goes with the idea that outweighs preventing over curing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the regulations and promptly submit the accounts and transactions within the reglementary period in order for COA to render a timely audit of all transactions and reports of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce complete and proper accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommended that Management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay of P3,500.00 under the GAA be observed.
We also recommended that Management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deductions from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions unless it is classified as a thrift bank. Also, require the personnel involved to transact directly with the private institutions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should exert effort and install strategies to fast tract settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following remedial measures:

a)      Payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.

 b)    Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts.  .  In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting department has been diligent enough to collect these cash advances thru payroll deduction or reimbursement claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, Management direct officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded.

Likewise, we recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant  Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council.

We further recommended that the guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF as prescribed under DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 should be strictly adhered to.

The accountant should make correction/adjusting entries on erroneous entries made on the purchase of rescue equipment and on other purchases.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive shall require the inventory committee to conduct complete physical inventory of its property to check the existence, physical condition and accountability   and ascertain the validity and correctness of the account balance in the financial statements and submit a report thereon to the Auditor concerned, in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer and Accountant shall maintain and update their property cards and equipment ledger cards, respectively, to facilitate the inventory taking and reconciliation between the property and accounting records.  Likewise, Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) should be issued to officials and employees to pinpoint accountability and responsibility.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) personnel follow the rules and regulations in processing the monetization of leave credits.We also recommended that limitation on the number of days be observed in the monetization of leave credits to give chance for the others to monetize and so that there will be available funds for  the others who want to claim their termination/separation pay.

We also required the Municipal Budget Officer to explain the difference of P233,531.44, as to where this funds were taken from to pay for the monetization.

We recommended the following:
 a. That Management direct Ms. Laniton to report to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and function as the Assistant Municipal Treasurer as appointed; and

b.That Ms. Laniton refund the total RATA amount she received while performing the work other than as the Assistant Municipal Treasurer in the total amount of P78,400.00.
observations.managementAction: Management has committed to comply with this observation. As of December 31, 2017, P43,754.00 was already refunded by Ms. Laniton.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU-LagonglongManagementtake appropriate actions on these observations.

Management, however, committed to follow up this matter. As of December 31, 2017, P564,062.50 was already refunded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management appropriated funds from the General Fund totaling P742,854.70 and thereafter direct the Budget Officer and the Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments affecting the transactions listed above, and restitute the said amount to the 20% DF which was depleted due to improper charging thereof.

We also recommended that Management observe the proper utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to attain the government’s objectives.

Recommendations:

Recommendation:
We recommended therefore that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated DRRM Officer to prioritize the preparation and submission of the LDRRMFIP, and the maintenance of public awareness through bulletin board.  The submission, and compliance of, these reports has basic importance on the lives of the constituents as it promotes disaster mitigation, prevention, and preparedness.
In addition, the Designated DRRM Officer must consider the prompt fulfillment of the activities or tasks enumerated in the LDRRMFIP as the most effective way of handling disaster goes with the idea that outweighs preventing over curing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the regulations and promptly submit the accounts and transactions within the reglementary period in order for COA to render a timely audit of all transactions and reports of the Municipality.
Management Comments:

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management strictly enforce complete and proper accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay of P3,500.00 under the GAA be observed.
We also recommended that Management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deductions from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions unless it is classified as a thrift bank. Also, require the personnel involved to transact directly with the private institutions.
Management Comments:
The Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer comply with the above cited provisions in the proper preparation of the cashbooks and the maintenance of records in order to provide a reliable report on her accountability. Dates of collections and deposits should be indicated; deposits should be recorded on the month it was deposited; cashbooks should be balanced daily; it should be indicated in the RCD from which collections or accountable officer the amount to be deposited is from; remittances of CTC collections of barangay treasurer should be recorded individually, not lump sum; and the Municipal Treasurer should maintain her own files of RCDs to support her cashbooks.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer employ adequate control measures to ensure that cash and accountable forms are free from the risk of loss or misappropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should exert effort and install strategies to fast tract settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following remedial measures:

a)      Payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.

 b)    Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts.  .  In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.
observations.reason: The Accounting department has been diligent enough to collect these cash advances thru payroll deduction or reimbursement claims.
Under the new administration, the Management has committed to comply with this audit observation memorandum.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) personnel follow the rules and regulations in processing the monetization of leave credits. We also recommended that limitation on the number of days be observed in the monetization of leave credits to give chance for the others to monetize and so that there will be available funds for  the others who want to claim their termination/separation pay.

We also required the Municipal Budget Officer to explain the difference of P233,531.44, as to where this funds were taken from to pay for the monetization.
observations.reason: Under the new administration, the Management has committed to comply with this audit observation memorandum.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the charging of those expenditures already provided in the DepEd budget to the SEF of the Municipality and observe strictly the rules and regulations in the utilization of SEF as provided in the DECS-DBM-DILG JC No.1.

We recommended the following:
 a. That Management direct Ms. Laniton to report to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and function as the Assistant Municipal Treasurer as appointed; and

b. That Ms. Laniton refund the total RATA amount she received while performing the work other than as the Assistant Municipal Treasurer in the total amount of P78,400.00.
observations.reason: Management has committed to comply with this observation. As of December 31, 2016, P23,754.00 was already refunded by Ms. Laniton.
